proposed_hypotheses.text: At least one of the 123 features in the dataset is associated (Spearman correlation or one-way ANOVA) with pfs_months at p<0.001; running a univariate screen will reveal a small set of strong predictors and a long tail of null features.

analyses.code: for c in features: spearmanr(df[c], df['pfs_months']) or f_oneway across category levels
analyses.result_summary: Out of 123 features, 18 reached p<0.05 and 10 reached p<0.001. The strongest signals were feature_078 (rho=+0.785), feature_057 (rho=-0.360), feature_051 (binary, mean diff -1.35), feature_038 (binary, mean diff +0.97), feature_099 (rho=-0.120), feature_092 (rho=+0.103), feature_013 (binary, -0.33), feature_009 (rho=-0.047), feature_043 (binary, -0.31), and feature_109 (binary, -0.23). The remaining ~110 features showed no meaningful main-effect association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_078 (continuous, range 30-90) is positively associated with pfs_months: higher values of feature_078 predict longer progression-free survival, and the relationship is approximately linear across the full range.

analyses.code: linregress(df['feature_078'], df['pfs_months']); pd.qcut(df['feature_078'], 5).groupby(...)
analyses.result_summary: Linear regression slope = +0.1765 months of PFS per unit feature_078; R^2 = 0.587. Mean pfs_months rises monotonically across quintiles of feature_078: 1.86 (Q1, <=56.5) -> 3.33 -> 4.30 -> 5.27 -> 6.83 (Q5, >73.5). The relationship is strikingly linear and dwarfs all other univariate effects.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_057 (ordinal, 0/1/2) is monotonically and negatively associated with pfs_months: each successive level (0 -> 1 -> 2) corresponds to a step decrease in mean PFS, consistent with a stage-like or grade-like clinical variable.

analyses.code: df.groupby('feature_057')['pfs_months'].mean(); spearmanr; f_oneway
analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months by level: 0 -> 5.27 (n=17,592), 1 -> 4.05 (n=24,971), 2 -> 2.91 (n=7,437). One-way ANOVA F=3,529, p~0; Spearman rho=-0.360, p~0. Levels show a clean monotonic decrease of roughly 1.2 months per step, strongly consistent with a stage variable.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_051 (binary, prevalence ~0.55) is negatively associated with pfs_months: patients with feature_051=1 have substantially shorter PFS than those with feature_051=0.

analyses.code: ttest_ind(df.loc[df.feature_051==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df.feature_051==0,'pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months: 3.70 if feature_051=1 vs 5.06 if feature_051=0 (delta = -1.35 months). Independent t-test t=-68.6, p~0. After adjustment for feature_078 and feature_057 in OLS the coefficient is -1.36 (SE 0.008), confirming the effect is not driven by confounding with the two strongest predictors.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_038 (binary, prevalence ~0.20) is positively associated with pfs_months: patients with feature_038=1 enjoy longer progression-free survival than those with feature_038=0, with an unadjusted gain of approximately 1 month.

analyses.code: ttest_ind on pfs_months by feature_038; OLS adjustment
analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months: 5.09 if feature_038=1 vs 4.12 if feature_038=0 (delta = +0.97 months). t=+38.5, p~0. In the multivariate model with the other top predictors, the adjusted coefficient is +0.94 (SE 0.010), so the effect survives adjustment. feature_038 is the strongest positive (favorable) binary marker in the dataset.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_099 (continuous, range 0-24.6, right-skewed) is negatively associated with pfs_months: higher values of feature_099 predict shorter PFS in a dose-response pattern.

analyses.code: spearmanr; pd.qcut(...).groupby('pfs_months').mean()
analyses.result_summary: Spearman rho=-0.120, p=5.6e-160. Mean pfs_months falls monotonically across quartiles of feature_099: 4.57 (lowest) -> 4.36 -> 4.18 -> 3.87 (highest), consistent with a continuous adverse marker (e.g., a lab value where higher = worse). The adjusted OLS coefficient was -0.076 per unit (p~0).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_092 (continuous, range 1.5-5.5, ~39 unique values, distribution centered ~3.8) is positively associated with pfs_months: higher feature_092 predicts longer PFS, suggesting a favorable continuous marker (plausibly a serum protein such as albumin).

analyses.code: spearmanr; quintile-grouped means; OLS
analyses.result_summary: Spearman rho=+0.103, p~0. Mean pfs_months rises monotonically across quintiles of feature_092: 3.98 -> 4.26 -> 4.30 -> 4.48 -> 4.63. After adjustment for feature_078, feature_057, feature_051, feature_038, the OLS coefficient is +0.47 per unit (SE 0.008), so a 1-unit increase in feature_092 is associated with an extra ~0.5 months of PFS independently of stage and other markers.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_013 (binary, prevalence ~0.42) is negatively associated with pfs_months, with a smaller magnitude than feature_051 but still highly significant.

analyses.code: ttest_ind
analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months: 4.12 if feature_013=1 vs 4.45 if feature_013=0 (delta = -0.33). t=-15.8, p=8.5e-56. Adjusted OLS coefficient = -0.33 (SE 0.008), confirming an independent unfavorable effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_043 (binary, prevalence ~0.35) is negatively associated with pfs_months in the same direction and similar magnitude as feature_013.

analyses.code: ttest_ind; OLS adjustment
analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months: 4.11 if feature_043=1 vs 4.42 if feature_043=0 (delta = -0.30 months). t=-14.3, p=5.2e-46. Adjusted coefficient = -0.29 (SE 0.008), independent of the larger predictors.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_009 (continuous, range 0.02-777, very right-skewed, mean 9.2) is weakly negatively associated with pfs_months: higher feature_009 predicts shorter PFS, consistent with a tumor-burden or inflammatory lab marker.

analyses.code: spearmanr; OLS adjustment
analyses.result_summary: Spearman rho=-0.047, p=1.3e-25. Adjusted OLS coefficient = -0.005 per unit (SE 0.0002), p~0. The per-unit effect is small but the wide range (0-777) means the population-level contribution is non-trivial.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months differs significantly across categories of feature_064 (race: white / black / hispanic / asian / other), reflecting either disparate biology or disparate care.

analyses.code: f_oneway across df.groupby('feature_064')['pfs_months']
analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months: white 4.32 (n=32,456), hispanic 4.31 (n=7,572), asian 4.31 (n=3,003), black 4.27 (n=5,996), other 4.41 (n=973). One-way ANOVA F=0.87, p=0.48 - no significant difference. Race appears balanced with respect to PFS in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months differs significantly across feature_018 (insurance: medicare / medicaid / private / uninsured), with private-insured patients having the longest PFS and uninsured the shortest, consistent with access-to-care disparities.

analyses.code: f_oneway across df.groupby('feature_018')['pfs_months']
analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months: medicaid 4.28, medicare 4.31, private 4.33, uninsured 4.25. Spread is only ~0.08 months. ANOVA F=1.47, p=0.22 - no significant difference. The hypothesis is refuted.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The five other multi-level ordinal features (feature_025, feature_075, feature_071, feature_026, feature_096, feature_033 - each 0..4 or 0..10) show monotonic associations with pfs_months similar to feature_057, suggesting they may also encode stage- or grade-like information.

analyses.code: spearmanr for each ordinal feature against pfs_months
analyses.result_summary: Spearman rho values: feature_025 +0.006 (p=0.15), feature_075 -0.002 (p=0.67), feature_071 -0.004 (p=0.37), feature_026 -0.006 (p=0.16), feature_096 -0.004 (p=0.33), feature_033 +0.003 (p=0.55). None are significant. feature_057 is unique among the multi-level integer features in showing a strong, monotonic association; the others appear to be noise variables in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A multivariate OLS model with the nine top univariate predictors (feature_078, feature_057, feature_051, feature_038, feature_099, feature_092, feature_013, feature_009, feature_043) explains a large fraction of the variance in pfs_months and yields independently significant coefficients for each.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + feature_057 + feature_051 + feature_038 + feature_099 + feature_092 + feature_013 + feature_009 + feature_043', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted R^2 = 0.857. All nine coefficients are highly significant (|t| >= 21). Estimates (SE): feature_078 +0.176 (0.0004), feature_057 -1.172 (0.006), feature_051 -1.362 (0.008), feature_038 +0.942 (0.010), feature_099 -0.076 (0.001), feature_092 +0.470 (0.008), feature_013 -0.327 (0.008), feature_009 -0.005 (0.0002), feature_043 -0.286 (0.008). Each effect is independent of the others.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The slope of pfs_months on feature_078 is steeper at low feature_057 (level 0) than at high feature_057 (level 2), i.e., the favorable feature_078 effect is partly attenuated in the highest-stage group (interaction feature_078 x feature_057 < 0).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 * C(feature_057)', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Slope of feature_078 on pfs_months: 0.180 at feature_057=0, 0.177 at feature_057=1 (interaction = -0.003, p=0.037), 0.163 at feature_057=2 (interaction = -0.017, p=8e-23). Statistically significant attenuation of the feature_078 effect in level 2, confirming the hypothesis but the magnitude is modest.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The unfavorable effect of feature_051 on pfs_months is larger in patients with low feature_057 (level 0) than in patients with high feature_057 (level 2); i.e., feature_051's adverse impact is partially saturated in the worst-stage subgroup (positive feature_051 x feature_057 interaction).

analyses.code: Stratified t-tests + smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_051 * C(feature_057) + feature_078', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Stratum-specific feature_051 deltas: feature_057=0 -> -1.40 (p~0); =1 -> -1.35 (p~0); =2 -> -1.27 (p=3e-164). Formal interaction terms vs level 0: feature_051:C(057)[1] = +0.044 (p=0.032); feature_051:C(057)[2] = +0.093 (p=0.001). The hypothesis is supported but the effect-size attenuation is small (~7% relative reduction at level 2).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The favorable effect of feature_038 on pfs_months is consistent across feature_057 levels in absolute terms but somewhat attenuated at the highest stage (negative feature_038 x feature_057 interaction).

analyses.code: Stratified means + smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_038 * C(feature_057) + feature_078 + feature_051', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Stratum-specific feature_038 deltas: feature_057=0 -> +0.93; =1 -> +1.00; =2 -> +0.90. Adjusted interaction terms vs level 0: feature_038:C(057)[1] = -0.066 (p=0.006); feature_038:C(057)[2] = -0.112 (p=0.001). The favorable effect of feature_038 is significantly though modestly attenuated at high feature_057 - clinically the benefit is preserved at all levels.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_038 and feature_051 act non-additively on pfs_months (a feature_038 x feature_051 interaction is present), e.g., the favorable effect of feature_038 is amplified or dampened depending on feature_051 status.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_038 * feature_051 + feature_078 + C(feature_057)', data=df).fit(); 2x2 mean table
analyses.result_summary: 2x2 mean pfs_months: (038=0,051=0)=4.87, (038=0,051=1)=3.50, (038=1,051=0)=5.81, (038=1,051=1)=4.51. Stratified feature_038 deltas: +0.94 within feature_051=0 vs +1.01 within feature_051=1. Adjusted interaction coefficient = +0.029 (SE 0.022, p=0.19) - not significant. The two effects are essentially additive, refuting the hypothesis.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The unfavorable effect of feature_051 on pfs_months grows with increasing feature_078 (negative feature_078 x feature_051 interaction), so the harmful impact of feature_051 is more pronounced at high feature_078.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 * feature_051 + C(feature_057) + feature_038', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted interaction coefficient feature_078:feature_051 = -0.0070 per unit feature_078 (SE 0.0009, t=-7.95, p=2e-15). The slope of pfs_months on feature_078 is +0.180 in feature_051=0 patients and +0.173 in feature_051=1 patients - i.e., feature_051 confers a small additional 'penalty' at higher feature_078. Hypothesis supported, though the absolute interaction is small.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The favorable effect of feature_038 on pfs_months scales with feature_078 (positive feature_078 x feature_038 interaction): patients with high feature_078 benefit more from feature_038=1.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 * feature_038 + C(feature_057) + feature_051', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted interaction coefficient feature_078:feature_038 = +0.0012 per unit feature_078 (SE 0.0011, t=1.10, p=0.27). Not significant. The favorable effect of feature_038 is constant across the feature_078 range; hypothesis refuted.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The favorable effect of feature_038 on pfs_months differs significantly across feature_064 race categories - in particular, white patients show a different magnitude of benefit than minority patients (race x treatment interaction).

analyses.code: Stratified means + nested anova_lm test of feature_038 x C(feature_064) interaction adjusting for feature_078 and feature_057
analyses.result_summary: Race-stratified feature_038 deltas: white +0.92, hispanic +1.11, asian +1.09, black +0.97, other +1.22. Nested F-test for interaction with race: F=2.12, p=0.076 (not significant at alpha=0.05). The favorable effect of feature_038 is statistically homogeneous across racial groups; hypothesis is refuted in this dataset.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The unfavorable effect of feature_051 on pfs_months differs across race (feature_064) - patients of different races experience meaningfully different magnitudes of harm from feature_051.

analyses.code: Stratified means + nested anova_lm test of feature_051 x C(feature_064) adjusting for feature_078 and feature_057
analyses.result_summary: Race-stratified feature_051 deltas: white -1.34, hispanic -1.39, asian -1.33, black -1.35, other -1.52. Nested F-test for interaction: F=0.31, p=0.87. Highly homogeneous across race; hypothesis refuted.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Insurance status (feature_018) modifies the favorable effect of feature_038 on pfs_months - specifically, privately-insured patients realize a larger benefit than uninsured or Medicaid patients (insurance x treatment interaction).

analyses.code: Stratified means + nested anova_lm test of feature_038 x C(feature_018) adjusting for feature_078 and feature_057
analyses.result_summary: Insurance-stratified feature_038 deltas: medicare +0.97, private +0.94, uninsured +0.93, medicaid +1.11. Nested F-test for interaction: F=1.89, p=0.13. Not significant; benefit of feature_038 is consistent across insurance categories. Hypothesis refuted (notably, private patients do not benefit more than uninsured/Medicaid patients).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Several of the small-effect or marginal univariate features (feature_109, feature_067, feature_028, feature_094, feature_103, feature_070, feature_089, feature_102) reflect genuine independent effects on pfs_months once the major confounders (feature_078, feature_057, feature_051, feature_038) are adjusted for.

analyses.code: For each f: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ f + feature_078 + C(feature_057) + feature_051 + feature_038', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coefficients (and p-values): feature_109 -0.205 (p=8e-23, SIGNIFICANT, ~2-week reduction in PFS), feature_067 +0.171 (p=2e-11, SIGNIFICANT), feature_089 +0.035 (p=0.029, marginal), feature_028 -0.016 (p=0.25, NS), feature_094 -0.003 (p=0.75, NS), feature_103 +0.002 (p=0.81, NS), feature_070 -0.000 (p=0.68, NS), feature_102 -0.004 (p=0.68, NS). Two of the eight features (feature_109 and feature_067) retain real independent signal; the rest are absorbed by the major covariates and are effectively noise.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The full multivariate model that combines the nine main effects (feature_078, feature_057, feature_051, feature_038, feature_099, feature_092, feature_013, feature_009, feature_043) plus the three statistically supported interactions (feature_078 x feature_051, feature_057 x feature_051, feature_057 x feature_038) provides a significantly better fit than a main-effects-only model, but the marginal improvement in adjusted R^2 is small (a few thousandths), indicating that PFS in this cohort is driven mostly by additive main effects with only modest effect-modification.

analyses.code: Nested anova_lm comparing main-effects-only OLS to OLS with three interactions added
analyses.result_summary: Main-effects model: R^2=0.8574, adj R^2=0.8574, AIC=127,522. Interaction-augmented model: R^2=0.8577, adj R^2=0.8576, AIC=127,425. Nested F-test (5 added interaction df): F=21.6, p=1.3e-21 - statistically the interactions matter, but the practical fit improvement is only ~0.0003 in adjusted R^2. Conclusion: PFS in this cohort is overwhelmingly explained by additive contributions of feature_078 (the dominant driver, ~58% of variance alone), feature_057, feature_051, and feature_038, with feature_099/feature_092 and the smaller binary markers contributing on the margin. Race and insurance show neither main effects nor effect-modification.
